skill.name: when-analyzing-skill-gaps-use-skill-gap-analyzer
skill.description: Analyze a skill library to find coverage gaps, overlapping skills, usage problems, and practical portfolio improvement recommendations.
skill.summary: Find skill library gaps, redundancies, and optimization opportunities.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak-cryptography and command-execution patterns, but the weak-cryptography alerts are false positives in documentation, graph labels, and sample analysis text. The confirmed risk is moderate because the skill instructs agents to run external npx claude-flow commands and scan Claude skill directories, including a hidden home directory path.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution in Skill Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs the agent to run npx claude-flow@alpha commands for hooks, memory storage, retrieval, and aggregation. This appears aligned with the skill purpose, but executing an external package can run code outside the model response and may fetch package code depending on the environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and repeated across the workflow. They are documented operational steps, not hidden code, so the concern is execution risk rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Local Skill Directory Scanning
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to scan ~/.claude/skills and project .claude/skills directories. This is necessary for portfolio analysis, but it may expose private skill names, prompts, and local configuration content to the analysis session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem access is directly stated and matches the skill objective. The risk depends on user consent and workspace boundaries, so it is moderate rather than high.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings match benign text such as SKILL.md references, graph layout attributes, Markdown examples, and sample priority labels. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation, hashing, encryption, or insecure algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these are documentation or diagram strings, not executable cryptographic code. The files are Markdown and Graphviz process documentation.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Contextual Inventory Steps
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance alerts are caused by examples that classify skills, track usage, or mention API and webhook capabilities. No evidence found of active network scanning, credential harvesting, or host enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms appear in examples and portfolio-analysis metrics. The reviewed context does not contain commands for network reconnaissance.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses npx claude-flow@alpha in example workflows. Users should review package trust and avoid running these commands automatically on sensitive systems.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact command pattern is present in executable shell blocks. It is a legitimate integration pattern, but it expands the trust boundary.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Home Directory Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs scanning of ~/.claude/skills. This hidden configuration directory can contain private local skills and prompts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path is explicit and repeated in the documentation. The concern is data exposure from local skill inventory, not stealthy filesystem access.

content.value_statement: Skill libraries can develop blind spots, duplicated workflows, and unused capabilities over time. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured portfolio review with prioritized recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Inventory skill metadata, tags, agents, complexity, and described capabilities.
Map coverage across domains such as development, DevOps, data, AI, security, and integration.
Detect redundant skills by comparing overlapping capabilities and similar descriptions.
Identify underused or overly complex skills using usage and success metrics when available.
Generate prioritized recommendations for new skills, consolidation, refactoring, and deprecation.
Produce a structured skill gap analysis report with metrics and expected impact.
limitations: It depends on accessible skill files and accurate metadata.
Usage metrics must already exist or be supplied by the user.
Overlap scoring is heuristic and needs human review before consolidation.
External claude-flow commands should be reviewed before execution.

use_cases.title: Quarterly Skill Portfolio Review
use_cases.description: Assess coverage, redundancy, and maintenance priorities across a growing internal skill library.

use_cases.title: New Skill Library Planning
use_cases.description: Identify core domains and missing capabilities before investing in new skill creation.

use_cases.title: Skill Consolidation Sprint
use_cases.description: Find overlapping skills and plan safer merges, refactors, or archive decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Gap Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small skill library
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my skill library for missing capabilities and duplicate skills. Summarize the top gaps and recommended next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Domain Coverage Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my skills by domain. Compare coverage for development, DevOps, data, security, documentation, and productivity workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Redundancy and Consolidation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Find skills with overlapping capabilities. Recommend which skills to merge, archive, or keep separate, with reasoning for each decision.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Portfolio Optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a comprehensive skill portfolio analysis using metadata and usage metrics. Prioritize gaps, redundancies, complexity issues, and expected impact.

output_examples.input: Analyze a 47-skill internal library for coverage gaps.
output_examples.output: A report shows data engineering as the largest gap, lists missing ETL and validation capabilities, and recommends creating one focused workflow skill.

output_examples.input: Find redundant code review skills before a cleanup sprint.
output_examples.output: A consolidation plan identifies four overlapping review skills, explains shared capabilities, and recommends one unified review orchestrator.

output_examples.input: Review usage metrics for underused or complex skills.
output_examples.output: An optimization summary highlights rarely used skills, high-token workflows, and refactoring actions ranked by effort and expected benefit.

best_practices: Review the generated recommendations with skill owners before deleting or merging skills.
Provide usage metrics when possible so optimization advice is evidence based.
Run the analysis after major product, workflow, or repository changes.
anti_patterns: Do not treat heuristic overlap scores as final deprecation decisions.
Do not scan private skill directories without user approval.
Do not run external CLI commands from the skill without reviewing their purpose.

faq.question: What does this skill analyze?
faq.answer: It analyzes skill metadata, described capabilities, usage signals, overlap, gaps, and improvement opportunities.

faq.question: Does it create new skills automatically?
faq.answer: No. It recommends new skills or changes, but users must approve and implement them.

faq.question: Can it work without usage metrics?
faq.answer: Yes, but optimization findings are stronger when usage, success rate, and token data are available.

faq.question: Why does it scan local skill directories?
faq.answer: It needs skill files to build an inventory and compare coverage across the library.

faq.question: Is this suitable for large teams?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows for quarterly reviews, consolidation planning, and portfolio governance.

faq.question: What should users review before running it?
faq.answer: Users should confirm which directories can be scanned and review any external CLI commands first.
